title: Logic, Sets, Counting, and Decision Rules · Decision Notebook: Venn Counting
summary: Audit eligibility rules and resource allocation without double-counting people or contradicting policy. Planned artifact: Add and verify the math beg venn counting increment in a tested eligibility and allocation rule set. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use Venn diagrams and inclusion-exclusion to count overlapping groups exactly once.
Represent categories, subsets, unions, intersections, and complements from precise definitions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

steps.title: Calculate Venn Counting evidence
steps.instruction: 29 records meet rule A, 22 meet rule B, and 11 meet both. How many meet A or B? Use the result to decide Venn Counting for a tested eligibility and allocation rule set.
steps.why: The Venn Counting result for a tested eligibility and allocation rule set is incomplete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Use inclusion-exclusion: |A ∪ B| = |A| + |B| − |A ∩ B|. Reconcile the result with this competency evidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use Venn diagrams and inclusion-exclusion to count overlapping groups exactly once.
